characterCraft.note: At native 160x360, the 11x16 hero is now subordinate to the 14px dungeon topology while retaining a readable face, hair, cape, separate gait, boots, sword arm, facing, slash, hurt, collapse, and admire poses. The 10x9 slime, 12x10 bat, 13x16 armored guard, and singular 26x27 horned warden form a deliberate cast hierarchy: ordinary actors stay near one tile and the boss remains nearly two tiles wide and tall without reading as an enlarged hitbox.

environmentCraft.note: The dungeon reads as a built overhead place with masonry walls, recessed floors, lintels, foreground courses, room thresholds, fogged unknown chambers, and role-specific landmarks. Shelves and astrolabes, forges and chains, sarcophagi and moon dials, then roots and fountains give each floor the prop density and material specificity visible in MACHINE HUNT and BLOCK MINE; key structures remain visibly larger than the reduced neutral cast.

levelVariety.note: The Scriptorium, Ember Forge, Moon Ossuary, and Verdant Reliquary replace wall, floor, landmark, light, ambient-motion, and act silhouettes rather than recoloring one room set. The native montage makes every floor recognizable without its title, and measured adjacent-floor structure distances all clear the executable composition floor.

animationImpact.note: The inspected deterministic 30-second autoplay clip shows continuous hero and enemy travel at the corrected body scale, with gait, weapon, cape, pursuit glints, and movement streaks remaining readable as presentation outside the neutral body envelope. It also shows a readable guard tell, displaced sidestep and blocked-lane brace responses, sword counters, sigil and vault payoffs, the full-screen FLOOR ROUTED apex, and a transition into a materially rebuilt floor.

readability.note: Numbered sigils visibly feed 0/2 and 1/2 vault states; the guard's ring/exclamation tell, charge streak, hero SIDESTEPPING or BLOCK response, and room-changing solve carry intent without a route overlay. The smaller hero and enemies improve room, corridor, and landmark legibility while fixed motion crops and stable actor anchors prove that locomotion remains authored rather than receiving credit from loose probe boxes.

artDirectionCohesion.note: Cream stone highlights, ink recesses, biome accents, gold sigils and rewards, cyan interaction energy, and coral danger form one consistent retro-adventure language across architecture, actors, local-state HUD, enemy tells, and payoffs. The corrected one-tile ordinary cast and larger landmark/boss silhouettes now reinforce the dense tiled world instead of competing with it, while remaining distinct from both references at the actual 160x360 viewing size.
